summary: A nuclear power, UN Security Council permanent member, and Europe's second-largest economy with an independent foreign policy tradition (Gaullist), a global cultural footprint, and Africa's most influential former colonial power.

history.detail: Monarchy overthrown; ideals of liberty, equality, fraternity spread globally.
Conquers most of Europe; reshapes law and borders.
France diminished; Congress of Vienna redraws Europe.
France defeated; Alsace-Lorraine lost to Germany.
1.4 million French soldiers killed; northern France devastated.
France falls in 6 weeks; Vichy regime collaborates with Germany.
Allied D-Day landings; de Gaulle leads Free France back to Paris.
De Gaulle creates strong presidency; current constitutional system.
14 African colonies become independent; French influence persists (Françafrique).
De Gaulle asserts strategic autonomy; rejoins full command 2009.
France central to creating the European Union.
ISIS attacks kill 130; Bataclan and November 13 tragedy.

finance.currency_role: EUR; France is second-largest eurozone economy
finance.central_bank: Banque de France (1800); member of ECB system
leverage_tools: CFA franc (pegged to EUR) — used by 14 African nations

hubs: Paris (Station F — world's largest startup campus)

resources.oil_gas: Minimal domestic; nuclear provides 70% of electricity
resources.nuclear: Largest nuclear share of electricity in world; EDF operates 56 reactors
resources.rare_earths: None domestically; EU-level dependency on China
resources.water: Good supply; Rhine, Loire, Seine basins
resources.agriculture: EU's largest agricultural producer; wine, wheat, dairy

follow_the_money.detail: 14 African countries use franc pegged to EUR; France holds 50% of reserves
EDF exports technology globally; key geopolitical leverage

theories_disputed.detail: Decades of covert French military and financial interference in Africa; some operations officially declassified.
DGSE (French intelligence) bombed Greenpeace ship in 1985. France admitted responsibility.

connections.detail: Franco-German motor of EU; Élysée Treaty
Lancaster House Treaties on nuclear cooperation
NATO ally; periodic tensions over autonomy
Sanctions post-Ukraine; historical Gaullist engagement now reversed
